natural_language: Seven blocks in two formations. The main structure is a four-block tower: from the table up, A is the base, then B, then C, and D caps the top. Only D is exposed and reachable right now. Three loose blocks — E, F, G — sit directly on the table, each with nothing on top. The gripper is empty. We have two objectives. First: reverse the four-block tower so it becomes A on B, B on C, C on D, with D now at the base on the table. Second: take those three loose blocks and build a mini-tower: E on F, F on G, with G as the foundation on the table. So after the rearrangement, you'll have two towers side by side: a reversed four-block pillar and a new three-block pillar. The loose blocks and the original tower blocks should not intermix — keep the original four together in their new arrangement, and use only E, F, G for the new tower.
